title: Weekly Dose of Optimism #199
gist: A roundup of optimistic tech/science developments: Stripe's $500M Intercept initiative against respiratory infections (co-funded by Anthropic and OpenAI Foundation), a veto-stalled bipartisan housing deregulation bill, a $17.5B DOE loan commitment for 10 Westinghouse AP1000 reactors, ultrasound brain-imaging breakthroughs, and funding to translate Soviet-era research papers as a startup-idea source.

insights.claim: Stripe launched Intercept, a $500M philanthropic initiative to eradicate respiratory infections, co-funded by Anthropic, The OpenAI Foundation, Flu Lab, and individuals from Jane Street.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Signals a wave of frontier-lab and fintech philanthropic capital pooling into underfunded biodefense/preventatives; watch for downstream funding of broad-spectrum antivirals and air-cleaning startups.
insights.quote: a $500M philanthropic initiative to make respiratory infections, like the common cold and flu, a thing of the past.

insights.claim: Respiratory infections kill 1M people a year and cost $600B annually in lost productivity, with most people spending roughly 5% of their lives sick from these viruses.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Frames a large, underfunded TAM that could attract venture and philanthropic capital into preventative biotech and air-quality tech.
insights.quote: they kill 1M people a year, cost $600B annually in productivity, and periodically threaten civilization through pandemics.
insights.locator: We treat respiratory infections as a minor nuisance

themes.theme: US Housing Policy and Build Deregulation

insights.claim: The House passed the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act 358-to-32, a veto-proof bipartisan majority that loosens federal building/lending regulations and sets new limits on institutional investors in the housing market.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Deregulation tailwind for homebuilders and construction-tech; new limits on institutional ownership could pressure single-family rental REITs and large landlords.
insights.quote: sets new limits on the role institutional investors can play in the market.
insights.locator: Congress Passes 21st Century Road to Housing Act

insights.claim: President Trump initially refused to sign the housing bill, leveraging it to push his SAVE America voter-ID legislation and stating he does not want to lower home prices for existing owners.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Execution/timing risk on housing-supply policy; political horse-trading can delay or dilute the build-more agenda even with veto-proof support.
insights.quote: nobody gives a f*ck about housing
insights.locator: To that end, President Trump is currently refusing

insights.claim: Not Boring Capital invested in Cuby to address the technical/construction side of cheaper housing, complementing the bill's regulatory push.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Construction-tech is positioned as the private-sector complement to housing deregulation; a thesis area for venture exposure.
insights.locator: Not Boring Capital invested in Cuby

insights.claim: The DOE Loan Program Office conditionally committed $17.5 billion to support construction of 10 Westinghouse AP1000 (1GW) reactors, structured as up to five loans each backing two reactors.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Large-scale government de-risking of gigawatt nuclear could revive the AP1000 supply chain (containment vessels, turbines) and utility partners; bullish for nuclear-exposed names.
insights.quote: conditionally commited $17.5 billion in funding to support the construction of 10 Westinghouse AP1000 reactors.
insights.locator: U.S. Bets Billions in Low-Cost Loans

insights.claim: Under the DOE structure, Westinghouse and each utility partner must commit $500M each ($1B total per project) upfront, after which DOE provides $3.5B in low-cost loans early to fund long-lead-time items.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: The upfront-equity-then-cheap-debt structure targets the highest-leverage delay point (procurement), a template that could be replicated to accelerate other capital-intensive infrastructure.
insights.quote: Both Westinghouse and the partner are required to fully commit their project equity, $500 million each ($1 billion total per project), upfront prior to accessing DOE loan funds.
insights.locator: EDF financing will support up to five loans

insights.claim: Advanced reactor startups are racing to criticality before July 4th 2026, with Aalo Atomics receiving DOE approval to turn on its Aalo-X reactor and Valar Atomics having demonstrated 24 hours of continuous operation at 100% power.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Advanced/small reactor companies are hitting real operational milestones, validating the SMR thesis and a policy-driven push to demonstrate US nuclear leadership.
insights.quote: Valar Atomics, whose criticality we covered last week, has gone on to generate power and then demonstrated 24 hours of continuous operations at 100% power.
insights.locator: Big win for the big reactors

insights.claim: Aleph Neuro obtained the highest-resolution 3D images of the human brain ever taken from outside the skull, using ultrafast ultrasound through the skull plus injected contrast microbubbles, with founders experimenting on their own brains.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Non-invasive ultrasound brain imaging is advancing rapidly toward brain-computer interfaces; a potential challenger path to invasive BCI approaches like Neuralink.
insights.quote: obtained the highest-resolution 3D images of the human brain ever taken from outside the skull

insights.claim: Ultrasound-based neurotech is clustering as a hot category, with Prophetic's dreaming headband, Midjourney's scanner, and Aleph Neuro's imaging all emerging in close succession.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: An emerging investable theme in non-invasive neurotech; watch ultrasound-based BCI as a distinct frontier from electrode implants.
insights.quote: Ultrasound is on an absolute tear.

themes.theme: Reviving Neglected Research as Startup IP

insights.claim: Analogue Group's Revival Fund is funding translation of ~15,540 Soviet papers (Doklady Akademii Nauk SSSR, 1957-1970) via SovietRxiv, premised on the idea that overlooked Soviet research is a source of buildable startup IP.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Surfacing forgotten/foreign research as a deal-sourcing strategy; a contrarian angle on where deep-tech defensible IP comes from.
insights.quote: an experimental fund dedicated to restoring neglected, illegible, or prematurely dismissed research to active circulation.
insights.locator: Analogue Group Funds Soviet Papers Translation

insights.claim: Longshot Space raised $20M (including $5M from South Park Commons) to build a ground-based hypersonic launcher derived from an old Soviet research paper, illustrating the Soviet-IP-to-startup thesis in practice.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Concrete proof point that old Soviet research is fundable into venture-scale companies; a cheap-launch alternative to conventional rockets worth tracking.
insights.quote: We're building a ground-based launcher that fires payloads to hypersonic speed, and eventually to orbit, for a fraction of what rockets cost.
insights.locator: Poetically, the same day that Aish announced
